criteria: Translate pyproject.toml and requirements.txt to package.json with correct dependencies and tsconfig.json with ES2022 strict mode, commonjs output, and outDir: ./dist.
Map Python's src/ package layout with __init__.py barrel exports to TypeScript's src/ with direct ES module imports — no unnecessary barrel index.ts files.
Translate Python dataclasses to TypeScript classes with proper constructor signatures, property declarations, and options-object pattern for optional parameters.
Map Python's Optional[T] to TypeScript T | null consistently, and dict[str, Any] to Record<string, unknown> rather than Record<string, any>.
Translate Python custom exception classes (InvalidJobStateError, QueueFullError, DuplicateJobError) to TypeScript custom Error subclasses with proper prototype chain (Object.setPrototypeOf).
Preserve error message content and format — state transition errors include the current status value, queue errors include the max size.
Translate asyncio.wait_for timeout pattern to Promise.race with setTimeout-based rejection and proper timer cleanup in finally block.
Translate asyncio.to_thread for CPU-bound work to appropriate TypeScript async pattern — synchronous execution within Promise.resolve is acceptable given the computational load.
Replace structlog with idiomatic structured logging using console.log with JSON-formatted context objects — no unnecessary third-party logging library.
Replace aiofiles with native fs/promises for async file I/O. Avoid unnecessary third-party dependencies where Node.js built-ins suffice. Final package.json should have zero runtime dependencies.

Job state machine transitions produce identical results: PENDING→RUNNING→COMPLETED, PENDING→RUNNING→RETRYING (with retry count), PENDING→RUNNING→FAILED (retries exhausted), PENDING→CANCELLED, and all invalid transition rejections.
TaskQueue push/pop respects priority ordering with FIFO tiebreaker. MinHeap implementation used — not Array.sort(). Queue capacity limits and duplicate rejection match Python behavior exactly.

CpuWorker produces identical outputs: factorial(20)=2432902008176640000, fibonacci(30)=832040, prime_check(97)=true, prime_check(100)=false. Input validation rejects negative values with matching error messages.
IoWorker echo, transform (uppercase/lowercase/identity/keys_only), and aggregate (with numeric filtering, empty list handling, mixed types) all produce identical outputs to Python.
Serialization roundtrips (serializeJob/deserializeJob, serializeResult/deserializeResult, serializeManyJobs/deserializeManyJobs) preserve all fields including enums, tags, and nested payloads.

TypeScript code uses camelCase for variables/functions, PascalCase for classes/types/enums, readonly for immutable config properties, and get accessors for computed properties (duration, isTerminal, isEmpty, isFull).

Proper TypeScript patterns used: abstract classes for StorageBackend/BaseWorker, string enums for JobStatus, numeric enums for JobPriority, union types for nullable fields, and no use of any where unknown or concrete types are inferrable.
Threading locks correctly removed — Node.js single-threaded event loop makes them unnecessary. asyncio.sleep translated to setTimeout wrapped in Promise with correct millisecond conversion.

Validation utilities match exact Python logic: validatePayload checks required keys, validatePriority accepts 0-10, validateJobName enforces length and character rules, sanitizeTags deduplicates and normalizes to lowercase.
MemoryStorage save/load/delete/exists/listKeys all match Python behavior including copy semantics (mutations to original object don't affect stored data) and sorted key listing with prefix filtering.
Compiled output in dist/ is loadable via require() — commonjs module format enables subprocess-based behavioral testing via node -e.
